page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 180 dedicated vCPUs derived from 90 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, running two threads per core. The system is equipped with 1440.0 GB of RAM, yielding a resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU evaluations, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 124350.65. Memory performance is highly rated, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 13485.99 GB/sec and uncached large-block read bandwidth at 349.44 GB/sec. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are supported by hardware flags including AVX-512 and VAES, contributing to top-tier PassMark encryption and extended instruction test results.

This configuration is highly effective for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database operations, and software compilation, as evidenced by its top-tier Geekbench clang compilation score. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference, particularly in prompt processing for 135M and 7B models, and text generation for 7B and 70B models. However, the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage solutions, which represents a key architectural tradeoff for I/O-heavy applications.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and 1440.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 90 physical cores, this x86_64 architecture instance delivers top-tier performance in multi-core CPU benchmarks and memory bandwidth, reaching 13485.99 GB/sec for cached small-block reads. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. The server excels in memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and software compilation, supported by advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and VAES. Benchmark results highlight strong capabilities in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation, making it suitable for large-scale enterprise applications, memory-bound computations, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server featuring 180 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 1440 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-180 is a memory-optimized server with 180 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 1440 GB RAM, designed for high-throughput workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring 180 dedicated vCPUs and advanced vector instructions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 180 dedicated vCPUs and 1440 GB of RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors.
Top-tier memory bandwidth with 13485.99 GB/sec cached small-block read speeds.
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512, VAES, and SHA_NI.
Strong performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation.
No local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external storage solutions.
